channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Huawei at center of European Parliament bribery investigation
item.description: Huawei is at the center of an investigation into alleged bribery and corruption within the European Parliament. Belgian Federal Police raided 21 locations across Belgium and in Portugal on Thursday morning and made arrests in Belgium and France as part of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: VergeIO enhances VergeFabric network virtualization offering
item.description: VergeIO is looking to shake up the virtual infrastructure market with its approach to virtualization and software-defined networking. VergeIO is not a startup; the company was founded in 2010 and hasn’t made huge waves in the market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don't click on those road toll texts. FBI issues fresh warning about the smishing scam
item.description: The FBI is warning Americans not to respond to scam road toll collection texts after receiving more than 2,000 complaints this month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Monkey Trial: A Look Back at the Scopes Trial
item.description: The Scopes trial, held a century ago, was a landmark case over the teaching of evolution in public schools.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No, your printer isn't possessed: a Windows 11 23H2 bug could be making it print random characters when connected via USB
item.description: It might seem to be a strange phenomenon at first, but if your printer has been cranking out random characters lately, it's because of Windows 11 23H2.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft faces FTC antitrust probe over AI and licensing practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) is reportedly pressing ahead with an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, a move that could reshape competition in AI and productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: T200: Empowering women to build C-level IT careers
item.description: CIOs and other digital leaders play a critical role in shaping the future of their organizations. Now more than ever, future-proofing the business requires developing a robust pipeline of IT leaders prepared to step up as the next generation of strategic partners and innovation anticipators.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google DeepMind Unveils Gemma 3: A Breakthrough in Generative AI Models
item.description: Google DeepMind introduces Gemma 3, a new generative AI model that excels in multi-modality and supports vision-language inputs and text outputs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel taps semiconductor veteran Lip-Bu Tan as new CEO
item.description: Intel today announced its new CEO is Lip-Bu Tan, a semiconductor industry veteran who has focused on software but nonetheless is very familiar with Intel. Tan previously was CEO of Cadence Design Systems, which makes software products used to design and verify complex electronic systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple faces UK antitrust threat as regulator targets browsers
item.description: Apple faces a renewed threat in the UK, where a new report from the top antitrust regulator says both it and Google are holding back innovation in the mobile browser market. By far, most of the criticism is being leveled at Apple.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: FTC asks judge to delay Amazon trial due to resource constraints
item.description: HALELUYA HADERO The Federal Trade Commission asked a federal judge on Wednesday to delay a trial in a case accusing Amazon of using deceptive practices in its Prime subscription program, citing staffing and budgetary challenges at the government agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: 2 tasty new tab tricks for Chrome on Android
item.description: Quick: How many tabs do you have open in Chrome on your Android phone right now? If the answer is zero, you’re an animal. Seek immediate psychiatric help. If the answer is one to two, you’re obsessively organized and a model Android device owner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Altera targets low-latency AI edge applications with new FPGA products
item.description: Altera has introduced the latest family of Agilex FPGAs, along with Quartus Prime Pro software, and FPGA AI Suite to enable the rapid development of highly customized embedded systems for use in robotics, factory automation systems, and medical equipment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Nebraska Legislature approves measure classifying gig economy workers as independent contractors
item.description: The Nebraska Legislature has approved a measure classifying some gig economy workers as independent contractors, according to its website. L.B. 229, introduced by state Sen. Bob Hallstrom, was approved Monday, with 33 voting in favor and 15 opposed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Delivering Secure Capabilities Faster: Navy's Approach to Cybersecurity
item.description: The Department of the Navy is rethinking its approach to cybersecurity and capability delivery, emphasizing innovation and collaboration with operators and industry partners.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Observe links end-user experience with back-end troubleshooting
item.description: Observe has bolstered its observability platform with frontend monitoring capabilities that it says will enable developers and IT teams to gain visibility into application performance on end-user browsers and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding
item.description: "The Trump administration's efforts to reduce the size of the federal government have resulted in decreased funding for biomedical research," said Sandra Babosu The post Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk says X outages caused by cyberattack
item.description: Musk hypothesized on his platform that "Either a large, coordinated group and/or a country" were responsible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Databricks’ new updates aim to ease gen AI app and agent development
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks is introducing four new updates to its portfolio to help enterprises have more control over the development of their agents and other generative AI-based applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KREX 5
